authors: Deer TR, Levy RM, Kramer J, Poree L, Amirdelfan K, Grigsby E, Staats P, Burton AW, Burgher AH, Obray J, Scowcroft J, Golovac S, Kapural L, Paicius R, Kim C, Pope J, Yearwood T, Samuel S, McRoberts WP
title: Dorsal root ganglion stimulation yielded higher treatment success rate for complex regional pain syndrome and causalgia at 3 and 12 months: a randomized comparative trial
study_question: Evaluate DRG stimulation safety/efficacy versus SCS for CRPS/causalgia in lower limbs.
population_assessed: 152 subjects diagnosed with complex regional pain syndrome or causalgia in the lower extremities.
outcome_measures: Treatment success (≥50% VAS reduction, no neurological deficits), safety, quality of life.
inclusion_criteria: Chronic intractable lower limb pain (CRPS/causalgia) ≥6 months, VAS >60mm, failed 2 prior pharmacologic treatments.
answer_to_study_question: DRG stimulation provided higher treatment success with less postural variation in paresthesia intensity compared to SCS.
